acceptance_targets: [CH10-PROSE-01] PASS if the cited passages no longer use the same negation-and-reclassification pattern more than twice across the chapter and each revised beat is distinguishable by scene-specific language alone; FAIL if three or more of those turns still rely on the same "It was not..." sentence engine.

[CH10-PROSE-02] PASS if the cited passages differentiate memory, embarrassment, suspicion, and threat through distinct bodily or environmental cues, with no more than two generic pause-marker or explanatory "as if" beats left among those moments; FAIL if the revised passages still reuse the same pause/simile scaffold across multiple different turns.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the cited passages no longer use the same negation-and-reclassification pattern more than twice across the chapter and each revised beat is distinguishable by scene-specific language alone; FAIL if three or more of those turns still rely on the same "It was not..." sentence engine.
findings.problem: The chapter repeatedly lands recognition beats through the same negation-and-reclassification engine: "It was not..." followed by a corrective label. Used this often, the hotel confession beat, the morning-after recalibration, the LLC discovery, and the alley détente start to sound mechanically related even when the underlying pressures differ.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the turns at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:105, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:170, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:172, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:232, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:387 so at least three of them land through direct positive statement, image, or action specific to that scene rather than the recurring "It was not X / it was Y" pivot.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the cited passages differentiate memory, embarrassment, suspicion, and threat through distinct bodily or environmental cues, with no more than two generic pause-marker or explanatory "as if" beats left among those moments; FAIL if the revised passages still reuse the same pause/simile scaffold across multiple different turns.
findings.problem: Several key turns are mediated through the same stock scaffolds: generic pause markers ("for a second") and explanatory "as if" comparisons. Those constructions recur often enough that memory flash, social hesitation, Ben's exposure, and the final surveillance atmosphere start to arrive through the same pre-translated sentence move instead of through freshly observed detail.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:83, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:101, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:129, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:200, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:272, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:302, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:379, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:425, replace several of those pause/simile beats with scene-bound physical or sensory reactions so each hesitation carries its own texture rather than the chapter's default scaffolding.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Focus on prose and copyedit finish: diction precision, rhythm control, sentence-level clarity, grammar, line-level polish without flattening style, and explicit aesthetic-risk audit (sanitization is the primary risk — do not soften or euphemize; push toward creative risk and specificity).
revision_pass.label: prose/copyedit fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the absurdity and cruelty of graduate labor without converting the book into caricature or chase-plot menace. In edge cases, prefer the messier and more compromising version of events if it yields stronger human consequence and remains causally plausible; render sex, professional theft, bureaucratic harm, and emotional pettiness with the same specificity as any technical problem.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene turns vague at the exact point humiliation, erotic charge, class shame, visa fear, or institutional cruelty becomes concrete, rewrite until the cost is materially and socially legible.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High; contractions are the baseline in speech, and uncontracted forms should appear only for deliberate precision, class performance, emotional emphasis, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate public and private pressure, ensemble chapters and solitary ones, bureaucratic scenes and domestic scenes, deadline compression and aftermath inventory, so no two adjacent chapters rely on the same structural rhythm or dialogue density.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary literary English grounded in material detail; technical language should appear only when the social reality of the lab requires it, and it should never replace bodily or relational consequence.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person centered on Leela, with occasional brief widenings that register group mood and institutional atmosphere without leaving her interpretive frame.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate compressed observational lines with longer accrual sentences that can carry comedy, shame, and procedural dread in the same movement; quiet chapters should breathe, deadline chapters should tighten without turning telegraphic.
